focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Detective Kincaid reveals a note linking Sidney Prescott to her mother's unsolved murder, warning that Sidney's life is in danger and urging Gale to help find her, though Gale claims she doesn't know Sidney's whereabouts.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Subject of a killer's threat linked to her mother's murder
timeline_nodes.salient_development: A note left at a murder scene connects Sidney to her mother's murder and indicates her life is in danger
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sidney is in hiding, keeping herself very well hidden
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The killer is targeting Sidney, and her mother's murder remains unsolved
evidence_quotes: Her life may be in grave danger.
I've tried to get in touch with Sidney Prescott, but she's keeping herself very well hidden.

relation_updates: Reinforces connection to Maureen Prescott's murder case
status_updates: Life is in danger from a new killer
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Daughter of Maureen Prescott, now threatened by a killer

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sidney wakes from a nightmare of her mother's ghost and a violent apparition breaking the window, but finds the window intact; her dog is barking, and she leaves the room.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Has a nightmare where her mother's ghost appears at the window, causing distress
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Wakes from nightmare, sees window is intact, and leaves the room
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Distress from nightmares about her mother
evidence_quotes: Then she sees, and her face loses any trace of color. MAUREEN PRESCOTT'S GHOSTLY WRAITH is pressed up against the window.
Sid's hysterical crying.
AWAKENING IN BED FROM HER NIGHTMARE. Her dog's barking wildly. THE WINDOW is fine.
SID crawls out of bed, disappearing out of the room.

relation_updates: Deepens unresolved relationship with Maureen Prescott's memory
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Haunted by her mother's memory, as shown in the nightmare

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sidney receives a threatening call from a killer linked to her mother's murder, who taunts her about past crimes and warns of a new attack in Hollywood targeting Dewey, prompting Sidney to arm herself.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Crisis counselor receiving a threatening call from the killer.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Killer calls, reveals knowledge of Sidney's identity, taunts about past murders, and threatens Dewey, prompting Sidney to arm herself.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Trembling after the call, armed with a .38 special, and peering out her office window into the dark night.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Killer's identity and the specific plan to target Dewey in Hollywood.
evidence_quotes: SIDNEY (into headset) This is Laura in Monterey reporting in for work.
WOMAN(O.S.) Don't you want to know how you can help, Sidney?
VOICE I want to finish what I started. I want to finish the game.

relation_updates: Adversarial relationship established with mysterious caller
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Takes immediate action to arm herself for self-defense.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Detective Kincaid reveals the killer left a photo of Sidney's mother at crime scenes; Sidney, upset she wasn't told, insists on visiting the studio where the crimes occurred.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sidney learns the killer left a photo of her mother at crime scenes and insists on visiting the studio to investigate.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To visit the studio and investigate the killer's connection to her mother.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sidney is determined to go to the studio to confront the situation.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Why the killer is targeting her mother's memory.
evidence_quotes: Sidney notices the clippings and the photograph of Maureen.
DETECTIVE KINCAID The killer leaves a newspaper clipping at each murder, Sidney. Recently he's also left a photo of your mother.
SIDNEY Why didn't you tell me? This is my mother, Dewey. Why didn't you say something?
SIDNEY Listen, Detective, I wanna see this studio where all this shit's going down.

relation_updates: Shares warm hug with Gale, affirming positive relationship
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Driven to uncover the truth about her mother.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sidney evades two ghostly attackers with knives, fights back, and escapes through a high window as they close in.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Uses a gravity knife to stab an attacker in the shoulder and escapes through a ceiling window as two attackers close in
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Escaped from the bathroom via a window, armed with her knife
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The two attackers remain unidentified and are still at large
evidence_quotes: Sid reaches into her pocket book, draws out a Gravity Knife. She flips open the blade,
Sid takes up her knife, buries it in her attacker's shoulder.
she leaps up, slides open the window, and dives out,

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Skilled in self-defense, demonstrated by quick knife use and evasion

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sidney reveals her attack as Gale and Jennifer uncover Sidney's mother was an actress and point to studio head John Milton as a new suspect, prompting plans to confront him.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Victim of an attack and learner of her mother's secret acting past
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Escapes an attack, reports it to police, and discovers her mother was an actress linked to studio head John Milton
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Under Detective Kincaid's protective custody, heading to the police station
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether John Milton is connected to the killers
evidence_quotes: They tried to kill me in the bathroom! The killers!
Sidney falls into Dewey's arms, collapsing like a house of cards, crying hysterically.
Sid, get in my car. You're coming back to the station with me where it's safe.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Emotionally overwhelmed and processing new information about her mother

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ghost kills Kincaid, threatens Gale, and declares her bait for Sidney as she panics and cries.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Indirect target as killer uses Gale as bait
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ghost kills Kincaid and takes Gale hostage, declaring her as bait for Sidney
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To rescue Gale and confront the killer
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sidney faces an immediate threat with Gale held hostage by the killer
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Gale's safety and the killer's plan to lure Sidney
evidence_quotes: The GHOST comes charging out of nowhere, slashes open Kincaid's throat, stabs him in the chest.
VOICE I need you as bait. For Sidney.
Gale closes her eyes, cries as the killer stalks towards her.

relation_updates: Gale's hostage situation intensifies obligation to act
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Protective of friends, motivated to act against the killer

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sidney receives a threatening call demanding she come alone to save Dewey and Gale, discovers Wallace's gun, takes it, and prepares to leave after being taunted about her mother's killer.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Chooses to confront killer alone to save Dewey and Gale
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Receives a threatening call demanding she come alone, decides to go, and takes Wallace's gun
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To meet the killer alone and ensure Dewey and Gale survive
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sidney has taken Wallace's gun and exited the office, preparing to go to the killer's location
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The killer's identity and the safety of Dewey and Gale
evidence_quotes: GALE (screaming) Sidney, stay away!
She pulls up a fully loaded handgun. That's the ticket.
She closes the drawer, slinks out the side exit.

relation_updates: Reaffirms commitment to Dewey and Gale by risking herself
status_updates: Takes possession of gun, preparing for confrontation
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Willing to risk her life for her friends

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sid encounters the Ghost on a replica set, hears distorted voices from a device, and flees into a mock-up of her childhood home after a terrifying confrontation.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Victim fleeing from Ghost on film set
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tricked by Ghost using voice-changing device, confronts him, and escapes into a replica of her childhood home
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Inside mock childhood home with door closed, Ghost outside
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to escape the mock house while Ghost is pursuing
evidence_quotes: Sid screams, dropping the phone out of fright.
THE GHOST HE STANDS in the patio doorway. A monstrous version of the voice-changing box in hand.
She bolts across the darkened set, running straight up the front porch of a mock-up of her own childhood home.
She swings open the front door, slams it closed.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Inside a replica of her childhood home during chase

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sidney shoots Angelina and Roman's corpse in frustration, ending the confrontation as Gale focuses on the story; Sidney leaves the scene.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Captive confronting Roman Bridger, the revealed orchestrator
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Roman reveals he is her half-brother and the director who orchestrated the murders, including their mother's, by supplying motives and pointers to others.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Still held at gunpoint by Angelina, reacting with anger and disbelief to Roman's revelation and threats.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to escape captivity and stop Roman's plan to frame her using a recorded voice.
evidence_quotes: Angelina walks Sidney into the living room at gunpoint.
THE GHOST The other half of you!
ROMAN I'm a director Sid, I direct.
SIDNEY All the nightmares. All the hell I went through was because of you, you spineless bastard!

relation_updates: Roman revealed as half-brother and enemy
status_updates: Truth about mother's past and origin of attacks revealed
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Reacting to Roman's familial claim and his role in her past suffering.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sidney leaves peacefully as the sun rises; the scene pans over Hollywood, marking a fitting end to the trilogy.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Farewell exchange with Detective Wallace after the incident
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Says goodbye to Wallace and walks off through studio gates as the sun rises
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Departing the scene calmly at sunrise
evidence_quotes: SIDNEY Take care Detective. Give my regards to the wife and kids.
Sidney walks off through the studio gates, the sun rising overhead, as calm and as casually as if nothing's happened.
SIDNEY It was just something that's been itching to be completed for the past four years.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Expresses completion of a four-year pending matter

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Sidney Prescott is targeted by a killer linked to her mother's unsolved murder, prompting her to confront past trauma and arm herself. As she investigates, she learns about her mother's hidden past and faces direct attacks, culminating in the revelation that her half-brother Roman is the mastermind. After defeating the killers and rescuing allies, she moves on from the violence, achieving closure.
